license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.tb4eiqw
title: Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitory assay
author: Francisco Herrera

steps_raw.instruction: Aqueous extract (40 µL) was added to a 20 µL solution of ACE (Sigma) (100 mU/mL), and incubated at 37 °C for 5 min.
Subsequently, 100 µL of HHL prepared at a concentration of 0.3% in a mixture of 40 µmol potassium phosphate buffer (JT Baker, Mexico)

Later 300 µM sodium chloride buffer (JT Baker, Center Valley, Pennsylvania, USA), previously adjusted to pH 8.3 either with 1 M HCl (JT Baker) or 1 M NaOH (JT Baker), and incubated at 37 °C for 45 min.
The reaction was stopped by adding 360 mL of TT dioxane (Sigma) solution (3% w/v) and 720 mL of 0.2 M phosphate buffer (JT Baker) (pH 8.3).

After centrifuging the reaction mixture at 10,000 × g for 10 min, enzymatic activity was determined in the supernatant by measuring absorbance at 382 nm (Perkin-Elmer, Lambda XLS, UV/VIS spectrophotometer).

The tests were performed in triplicate and antihypertensive activity was quantified by a regression analysis of ACE inhibitory activity (%) versus phenolic compounds concentration in aqueous extract and defined as an IC 50 value, that is, the concentration of phenolic compounds required to produce 50% ACE inhibition under the described conditions.
For angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitory assay lisinopril (antihypertensive drug) was used as control.
